title: DLP - User Message App Check
content:
Check if the given message app exists and is configured and retrieve the user details from it.

Cortex XSOAR/XSIAM SOAR playbook from the Palo Alto Networks Enterprise DLP pack (Palo_Alto_Networks_Enterprise_DLP, xsoar-supported).

Inputs — UserEmail: The user email address that will be used through email communication in case both Slack and MS Teams are not enabled.; UserDisplayName: The display name of the user. Will be used in case MS Teams or Email are the message app.; UserMessageApp: The communication method with the user.
Can be one of the following:

- Slack
- Microsoft Teams
- Email

If you choose to set "Email", it's also possible to set the relevant email integration instance with the "SendEmailInstance" input..

Outputs — user_display_name: The user display name.; Slack.User: The user name returned from Slack..

Integrations/scripts used: IsIntegrationAvailable, Set, SlackV3, pan-dlp-update-incident.
raw_yaml:

version: -1
name: DLP - User Message App Check
description: Check if the given message app exists and is configured and retrieve the user details from it.

view: |-
  {
    "linkLabelsPosition": {
      "13_3_#default#": 0.17,
      "13_6_yes": 0.17,
      "16_4_MSTeams": 0.53,
      "1_2_yes": 0.43,

      "4_3_no": 0.11,
      "4_6_yes": 0.15,

    },

        "x": 260,

inputs:
- key: UserEmail
  value: {}
  required: false
  description: The user email address that will be used through email communication in case both Slack and MS Teams are not enabled.
  playbookInputQuery:
- key: UserDisplayName
  value: {}
  required: false
  description: The display name of the user. Will be used in case MS Teams or Email are the message app.
  playbookInputQuery:
- key: UserMessageApp
  value: {}
  required: false
  description: |-
    The communication method with the user.
    Can be one of the following:

    - Slack
    - Microsoft Teams
    - Email

    If you choose to set "Email", it's also possible to set the relevant email integration instance with the "SendEmailInstance" input.
  playbookInputQuery:
outputs:
- contextPath: user_display_name
  description: The user display name.
  type: unknown
- contextPath: Slack.User
  type: unknown
  description: The user name returned from Slack.
tests:
- No tests (auto formatted)
